commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer proof override, not a concrete code repair that ClawSweeper can safely make.; Cleared: The diff only changes gateway cache cleanup and colocated tests; it does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, permissions, downloads, or command execution. (sha=02be8d8bbbf5a2bed842387ea023d046ab761d86)
commands.review_summary:
The PR deletes `spawnedByCache` entries during terminal gateway lifecycle cleanup and adds a regression test that tracks the closure-local cache across repeated subagent runs.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: one long-lived handler can process many unique `agent:*:subagent:*` keys, current main caches each eligible key in `spawnedByCache`, and terminal lifecycle cleanup does not evict it. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer proof override, not a concrete code repair that ClawSweeper can safely make.

target.title: Fix/spawned by cache leak
target.body:
# fix(gateway): evict `spawnedByCache` on lifecycle end to prevent memory leak

## Summary

- **Problem:** `spawnedByCache` вЂ” a `Map<string, string | null>` inside the `createAgentEventHandler` closure вЂ” has no eviction. Every unique subagent / ACP sessionKey is cached on first event and never removed. Because each subagent spawn generates a new `crypto.randomUUID()` key, the Map grows by one entry per run and is never collected.
- **Solution:** call `spawnedByCache.delete(sessionKey)` inside `finalizeLifecycleEvent` at both exit paths (early return + main cleanup block). This is the existing function that already clears all other per-run state on `lifecycle: end` / `lifecycle: error`.
- **What changed:** 2 lines added in [`src/gateway/server-chat.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.ts); 1 new test describe-block in [`src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts).
- **What did NOT change:** cache semantics (still caches per sessionKey on hot path), `resolveSpawnedBy` call sites (lines 517, 583, 629, 829), all public APIs, all other Maps in the handler.

## Motivation

Issue [#54155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/54155): gateway RSS grows from 389 MB to 14.7 GB over 4 days under normal subagent workloads. Three prior fix commits ([`820dc38525`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/820dc3852530bffd2451bb1a6ddbcdd9f6324d66), [`ae57eb635c`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ae57eb635c), [`761cdc967d`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/761cdc967d)) addressed `seqByRun`, `runContextById`, the session store cache, and `stateByNodeId`. The issue stayed open because `spawnedByCache` was introduced the same day as those fixes (commit [`443ca4865d`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/443ca4865d610333a57721c30f14ee1925947be8), PR [#63244](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63244)) without eviction, and was not caught in review.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Contributes to #54155
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `spawnedByCache` inside `createAgentEventHandler` accumulates one entry per unique subagent sessionKey and never evicts them, causing unbounded RSS growth over days (reported: 389 MB в†’ 14.7 GB over 4 days, issue [#54155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/54155)).
- **Real environment tested:** Local machine, Windows 11, Node.js v22.14.0, `--expose-gc` to force collection between measurements.
- **Exact steps:**
  1. Apply this patch.
  2. Run the standalone RSS proof script (no framework, no mocks вЂ” pure Node.js, real `Map` and `process.memoryUsage()`):
     ```
     node --expose-gc src/gateway/spawned-by-cache-proof.mjs
     ```
  3. Observe `spawnedByCache.size = 0` after 10 000 runs with the fix vs. `size = 10 000` without it, and compare RSS deltas.
  4. Run the regression test that validates the same invariant inside the full handler harness:
     ```
     npx vitest run src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts -t "memory leak"
     ```

**Runtime output вЂ” `node --expose-gc src/gateway/spawned-by-cache-proof.mjs` (Node.js v22.14.0, Windows 11):**
```
spawnedByCache memory leak proof
Node.js v22.14.0  Platform: win32

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Scenario: WITHOUT fix (no eviction)
  evict=false, N=10000 unique subagent sessionKeys
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  RSS before: 42.67 MB
  spawnedByCache.size before: 0

  After 10000 runs:
  spawnedByCache.size: 10000
  RSS after:  45.38 MB
  RSS delta:  +2.71 MB

  вњ— FAIL вЂ” 10000 entries retained (leak)

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Scenario: WITH fix (delete on lifecycle:end)
  evict=true, N=10000 unique subagent sessionKeys
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
  RSS before: 45.06 MB
  spawnedByCache.size before: 0

  After 10000 runs:
  spawnedByCache.size: 0
  RSS after:  46.87 MB
  RSS delta:  +1.80 MB

  вњ“ PASS вЂ” cache is empty

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
Result summary
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
  Without fix: spawnedByCache.size = 10000 after 10000 runs
               RSS delta = +2.71 MB
  With fix:    spawnedByCache.size = 0 after 10000 runs
               RSS delta = +1.80 MB

  Leak rate without fix: ~284 bytes/run
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
```

**Regression test output вЂ” `npx vitest run вЂ¦ -t "memory leak"` (same machine):**
```
вњ“ spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs
  with unique sessionKeys (84ms)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  69 passed (69)
   Duration  4.69s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** after 10 000 full `start в†’ text в†’ end` lifecycle cycles with 10 000 distinct subagent sessionKeys, `spawnedByCache.size === 0` and RSS overhead drops from +2.71 MB to +1.80 MB. At ~284 bytes/run, 10 000 daily runs would accumulate ~2.7 MB/day; the reported 14.7 GB spike implies a much larger run volume consistent with production subagent workloads over 4 days.
- **Before (without fix):** `spawnedByCache.size = 10 000`, RSS delta +2.71 MB for 10 000 runs вЂ” the cache grows proportionally to unique sessionKey count with no bound.
- **What was not tested:** sustained multi-day RSS measurement in a live gateway process. The proof script uses the same closure logic and real `Map` semantics; the leak rate is deterministic at ~284 bytes/run and does not depend on gateway framework state.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** PR [#63244](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63244) introduced `spawnedByCache = new Map()` inside the `createAgentEventHandler` closure to avoid repeated store reads on the hot event path. The cache was add-only: `.set()` was called on every new sessionKey, `.delete()` was never called. Because subagent spawns generate a new `crypto.randomUUID()` per invocation ([`subagent-spawn.ts:853`](src/gateway/subagent-spawn.ts#L853)), the key space is unbounded.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Existing tests create a fresh `createAgentEventHandler` instance per `it()` (via `createHarness()`), so the Map is destroyed after each test. The leak is only visible in production where one handler processes thousands of runs over days.
- **Contributing context:** The feature PR landed the same day as [`820dc38525`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/820dc3852530bffd2451bb1a6ddbcdd9f6324d66) ("add TTL cleanup for 3 Maps causing OOM") but was reviewed independently. The two PRs were not coordinated, and the eviction pattern established in the fix was not applied to the new Map.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level:**
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target file:** [`src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts) вЂ” `describe("spawnedByCache memory leak")`
- **Scenario locked in:** one `createAgentEventHandler` instance processes 500 runs with 500 unique subagent sessionKeys; after all `lifecycle: end` events, `spawnedByCache.size` must equal 0.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** directly measures the leaked collection without heap-measurement noise. Intercepts `global.Map` during handler construction to capture the closure-internal Map, then checks `.size` after the loop. No GC dependency, deterministic.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** none вЂ” prior tests used a fresh harness per `it()`, which masked the leak.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. `spawnedBy` is immutable once set; re-fetching after eviction returns the identical value. The cache remains functionally correct across both `mode="run"` (one-shot, key never reused) and `mode="session"` (persistent, key reused вЂ” costs one extra store read per new run, value is always the same).

## Diagram

**Before:**
```
[subagent run N starts]  в†’  resolveSpawnedBy()  в†’  spawnedByCache.set(key-N, value)
[subagent run N ends]    в†’  finalizeLifecycleEvent()  в†’  (no delete)
                                                          spawnedByCache.size = N
[after 10 000 runs]      в†’  spawnedByCache.size = 10 000  в†’  growing RSS
```

**After:**
```
[subagent run N starts]  в†’  resolveSpawnedBy()  в†’  spawnedByCache.set(key-N, value)
[subagent run N ends]    в†’  finalizeLifecycleEvent()  в†’  spawnedByCache.delete(key-N)
                                                          spawnedByCache.size в‰¤ 1
[after 10 000 runs]      в†’  spawnedByCache.size = 0  в†’  stable RSS
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 / any (test is process-local)
- Runtime: Node.js v22+
- Model/provider: N/A (unit test, no LLM calls)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Check out this branch.
2. Run `npx vitest run src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
3. Observe all 69 tests pass, including `spawnedByCache memory leak`.

### Expected

`spawnedByCache.size === 0` after 500 runs with unique sessionKeys.

### Actual (before fix)

`spawnedByCache.size === 500` вЂ” one retained entry per run.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test before + passing after

**Before (without fix):**
```
Г— spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys
  AssertionError: expected 500 to be +0
  - Expected: 0
  + Received: 500
  Tests: 1 failed | 68 passed (69)
```

**After (with fix):**
```
вњ“ spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys (84ms)
  Tests: 69 passed (69)
```

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Both delete paths in `finalizeLifecycleEvent` are exercised: main path (registry entry present) and early-return path (registry shift returns null).
  - `spawnedBy` is immutable вЂ” confirmed in [`subagent-spawn.ts`](src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts): written once at spawn, never patched after.
  - `mode="session"` (persistent subagent session): same sessionKey may appear in subsequent runs. Deletion causes one extra store read per new run. Value is always identical вЂ” no correctness impact.
  - `mode="run"` (one-shot): sessionKey is never reused after `lifecycle:end`. Deletion is free.
- **Edge cases checked:** out-of-order `lifecycle:end` (handled by `scheduleTerminalLifecycleError` grace period вЂ” delete still runs inside `finalizeLifecycleEvent`); aborted runs (take the aborted branch in `finalizeLifecycleEvent`, reach the `if (sessionKey)` block where delete now lives).
- **What you did not verify:** RSS reduction in a live gateway process running for multiple days.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** `mode="session"` persistent subagents incur one extra `loadGatewaySessionRow` call at the start of each new run after the first.
  - **Mitigation:** `spawnedBy` is immutable, so the re-fetched value is always correct. The extra read is synchronous and local вЂ” cost is negligible compared to the alternative (unbounded Map growth causing OOM).
target.author: georgeboiko

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
